name: owned_threads_shared_across_containers
code:
proof fn phi_1_owned_threads_shared_across_containers(
    root_container: ContainerPtr,
    container_perms: Map<ContainerPtr, PointsTo<Container>>,
    c1: ContainerPtr,
    c2: ContainerPtr,
    t: ThreadPtr,
)
    requires
        container_perms_wf(container_perms),
        container_tree_wf(root_container, container_perms),
        container_perms.dom().contains(c1),
        container_perms.dom().contains(c2),
        c1 != c2,
        container_perms[c1].value().owned_threads@.contains(t),
        container_perms[c2].value().owned_threads@.contains(t),
    ensures
        true,
{
}
reason: No disjointness constraint on owned_threads across containers — two containers can claim the same thread simultaneously

name: no_child_imply_no_subtree_external_body
code:
proof fn phi_2_no_child_imply_no_subtree_external_body(
    root_container: ContainerPtr,
    container_perms: Map<ContainerPtr, PointsTo<Container>>,
    c_ptr: ContainerPtr,
)
    requires
        container_perms_wf(container_perms),
        container_tree_wf(root_container, container_perms),
        container_perms.dom().contains(c_ptr),
        container_perms[c_ptr].value().children@ =~= Seq::empty(),
    ensures
        container_perms[c_ptr].value().subtree_set@ =~= Set::empty(),
{
    no_child_imply_no_subtree(root_container, container_perms, c_ptr);
}
reason: no_child_imply_no_subtree is external_body — the claim that empty children implies empty subtree_set is trusted without proof, yet subtree_set_wf only constrains membership direction (subtree member implies ancestor in uppertree), not completeness

name: remove_container_no_resource_cleanup
code:
proof fn phi_3_remove_container_no_resource_cleanup(
    root_container: ContainerPtr,
    old_container_perms: Map<ContainerPtr, PointsTo<Container>>,
    new_container_perms: Map<ContainerPtr, PointsTo<Container>>,
    container_ptr: ContainerPtr,
    t: ThreadPtr,
)
    requires
        remove_container_ensures(root_container, old_container_perms, new_container_perms, container_ptr),
        old_container_perms[container_ptr].value().owned_threads@.contains(t),
    ensures
        !new_container_perms.dom().contains(container_ptr),
{
}
reason: Removed container's owned_threads/endpoints/procs are silently dropped — remove_container_ensures never transfers or validates resource cleanup

name: root_container_parent_unconstrained
code:
proof fn phi_4_root_container_parent_unconstrained(
    root_container: ContainerPtr,
    container_perms: Map<ContainerPtr, PointsTo<Container>>,
)
    requires
        container_perms_wf(container_perms),
        container_tree_wf(root_container, container_perms),
        container_perms.dom().contains(root_container),
        container_perms[root_container].value().parent.is_Some(),
    ensures
        true,
{
}
reason: container_root_wf constrains depth==0 but never asserts root.parent.is_None() — root can have a spurious parent pointer

name: owned_endpoints_shared_across_containers
code:
proof fn phi_5_owned_endpoints_shared_across_containers(
    root_container: ContainerPtr,
    container_perms: Map<ContainerPtr, PointsTo<Container>>,
    c1: ContainerPtr,
    c2: ContainerPtr,
    e: EndpointPtr,
)
    requires
        container_perms_wf(container_perms),
        container_tree_wf(root_container, container_perms),
        container_perms.dom().contains(c1),
        container_perms.dom().contains(c2),
        c1 != c2,
        container_perms[c1].value().owned_endpoints@.contains(e),
        container_perms[c2].value().owned_endpoints@.contains(e),
    ensures
        true,
{
}
reason: owned_endpoints Ghost field has no disjointness constraint despite source comments about scoped endpoint ownership within container subtrees
